# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

# app/mailers/application_mailer.rb
class ApplicationMailer < ActionMailer::Base
  default from: 'sender@example.com' # Replace with your verified sender email address in SendGrid
  layout 'mailer'
end

# app/mailers/example_mailer.rb
class ExampleMailer < ApplicationMailer
  def welcome_email(user)
    @user = user
    @url  = 'http://example.com/login' # Or your application's actual login URL

    mail(to: @user.email, subject: 'Welcome to Our Service!')
  end
end

# config/environments/development.rb
# Add these lines inside the `Rails.application.configure do` block
config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
  address:              'smtp.sendgrid.net',
  port:                 587,
  domain:               'localhost', # Can be 'localhost' for development
  user_name:            'apikey',
  password:             ENV['SENDGRID_API_KEY'], # Set this in your local .env file (e.g., using dotenv-rails)
  authentication:       :plain,
  enable_starttls_auto: true
}
config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true
config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
config.action_mailer.default_url_options = { host: 'localhost', port: 3000 } # Adjust port if different

# config/environments/production.rb
# Add these lines inside the `Rails.application.configure do` block
config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
  address:              'smtp.sendgrid.net',
  port:                 587,
  domain:               ENV['SENDGRID_DOMAIN'], # Your production domain, e.g., 'yourproductionapp.com'
  user_name:            'apikey',
  password:             ENV['SENDGRID_API_KEY'], # Set this in your production environment variables
  authentication:       :plain,
  enable_starttls_auto: true
}
config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = false
config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
config.action_mailer.default_url_options = { host: ENV['SENDGRID_DOMAIN'] } # Crucial for links in emails
